summary: Large decompilation/matching PR centered on src/melee/mn/mngallery.c, plus smaller matches and signature cleanups in gm_1601, grcorneria, and lbaudio_ax. The decomp.dev report recorded 19 new 100% matches, including nine mngallery functions, four gm_1601 functions, grCorneria_801DEC94, and three lbaudio_ax improvements; fn_802590C4 was improved to 99.19% but explicitly remained a difficult fuzzy/non-final match.
what_changed: Implemented a large block of mngallery.c behavior, including mnGallery_8025896C, mnGallery_80258A08, mnGallery_80258BC4, mnGallery_80258DBC, fn_80258ED0, mnGallery_802591BC, mnGallery_80259604, mnGallery_8025963C, and mnGallery_80259868.
Added a near-complete implementation of fn_802590C4 in mngallery.c; the author described it as around a 95% fuzzy match, while the bot later reported 99.19%.
Added menu/gallery support code using baselib camera, gobj, gxlink, jobj, sobj/sislib, controller input, movie playback via lbMthp, and audio calls through lbAudioAx.
Matched gm_801636D8 and gm_80163A3C as frame-count-to-hours/minutes/seconds/centiseconds helpers with typed u8 output pointers.
Matched gm_80163838 as a total frame accumulation helper over 0x19 entries, returning false if any required gmMainLib entry is unavailable.
Matched gm_80164430 as a stage unlock check using Stage_8022519C, NUM_UNLOCKABLE_STAGES, lbl_803B790C, gmMainLib_8015EDA4, and a bit mask test.
Matched grCorneria_801DEC94 as a Vec3 position check against stage blast zone offsets with a 30.0f margin and z threshold.
Reworked lbAudioAx_800263E8 control flow from goto labels into nested conditionals that still preserve allocation failure behavior and return NULL when the sfx/stage argument is out of range.
Matched lbAudioAx_80026510 with a small PAD_STACK(8) adjustment and simplified bool return based on count.
Changed lbAudioAx_80026F2C from returning u64 to void, removed an unused arr pointer, and wrote directly to lbl_804337C4[i] while consuming the computed mask.
Updated headers to replace vague signatures such as void*/int/UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with more precise types: u8 pointers in gm_1601, Vec3* in grcorneria, and void return for lbAudioAx_80026F2C.
smart_moves: The PR paired matched C implementations with header signature tightening, making future call-site work safer and reducing placeholder type drift.
mngallery.c used local anonymous struct layouts to document only the offsets needed for matching without overclaiming full struct names or global layout knowledge.
The author used targeted MWCC pragmas such as opt_propagation off, dont_inline, optimization_level 2, and opt_strength_reduction on around hard functions instead of applying broad file-wide changes.
PAD_STACK was inserted only where the diff evidence suggests stack-shape sensitivity: mnGallery_8025896C, mnGallery_80258DBC, mnGallery_802591BC, and lbAudioAx_80026510.
The gm_1601 time conversion helpers reused the same frame division pattern and nullable output-pointer guards, which preserves behavior while clarifying the API.
grCorneria_801DEC94 was reduced to a simple, typed Vec3/blast-zone predicate, replacing an UNK signature with a clear stage-boundary helper.
lbAudioAx_800263E8 preserved the allocation cleanup path by unlinking the newly created gobj if userdata allocation failed.
The author explicitly called out fn_802590C4 as not fully solved instead of presenting it as a clean match, which is useful evidence for future follow-up.
decomp_lessons: When matching menu code, expect heavy interaction between baselib GObjs/CObjs/JObjs/SObjs, controller input, text objects, audio state, and movie playback; include the specific baselib headers required rather than relying on placeholders.
For partially understood menu state, small local structs keyed to observed offsets can unblock matching while avoiding premature global struct definitions.

Frame counters in gm_1601 appear to convert game frames to h/m/s/cs using val / 60 for seconds and ((99.0f * (val % 60)) / 59.0f) for centiseconds; reuse this exact float expression when matching related time-display helpers.
Header cleanup is part of matching work: changing int/void* to u8/u8* and UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete types can be necessary evidence for both codegen and later decomp readability.
Control-flow reshaping from goto-based decomp guesses to structured nested conditionals can improve or finish tiny remaining matches, as shown in lbAudioAx_800263E8.
Do not trust a non-void inferred return if the matched function only mutates globals; lbAudioAx_80026F2C became void and directly updates lbl_804337C4.
Hard MWCC codegen issues may require compiler-specific research. A collaborator pointed to a fully matched MWCC tree as a possible reference for fn_802590C4-style problems.
Evidence is weak for reviewer intent beyond the MWCC reference comment; there were no inline review comments in this slice.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions keep address-based module prefixes, e.g. mnGallery_80258A08, gm_80163838, grCorneria_801DEC94, and lbAudioAx_80026F2C.
Functions with unknown semantics may retain fn_ prefixes, as with fn_80258ED0 and fn_802590C4.
Concrete engine/library APIs keep established names and capitalization, e.g. HSD_CObjSetCurrent, HSD_GObjPLink_80390228, GObj_Create, Stage_GetBlastZoneRightOffset, and lbMthp_8001F410.
Headers should use concrete argument and return types once known, replacing UNK_RET, UNK_PARAMS, int, and void* placeholders.
Global labels remain address-style when meaning is unknown, e.g. lbl_803B790C, lbl_80433710, and lbl_804337C4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used MWCC pragmas around sensitive functions: #pragma opt_propagation off for mnGallery_80258A08 and fn_802590C4, #pragma dont_inline on around mnGallery_80258D50, and #pragma optimization_level 2 plus #pragma opt_strength_reduction on for fn_802590C4.
Used PAD_STACK to force stack layout where needed.
Used local volatile on HSD_GObj* volatile gobj8 in fn_80258ED0-related local struct, likely to preserve expected loads/stores.
Preserved odd control-flow details such as a goto found label in gm_80164430 for matching.

Preserved exact constants important for codegen and behavior: 0x271000 gallery movie buffer size, 0x83D60 lbAudioAx cutoff, 0x19 gm loop count, 0x37 audio mask loop count, 30.0f blast-zone margin, and 99.0f/59.0f centisecond conversion.
Used direct indexed global writes in lbAudioAx_80026F2C rather than pointer iteration to finish the match.
For fn_802590C4, the author experimented extensively and requested help; a collaborator suggested comparing against a fully matched MWCC source tree.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.summary: Reported matched code increase to 56.15% (+0.16%, +6052 bytes) and matched data increase to 37.51% (+24 bytes), with 19 new matches.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.summary: Reported fn_802590C4 improved from 0.00% to 99.19% but still unmatched; also reported mngallery .sdata2 at 80.00% and a small grcorneria .sdata2 regression.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.summary: Suggested a fully matched MWCC tree at https://git.wuffs.org/MWCC/tree/?h=main as a possible reference for compiler/codegen research.

review_feedback.author: human_review
review_feedback.summary: No inline review comments or formal review feedback were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Search for later PRs that finish or rename fn_802590C4.
Search for later struct definitions replacing the local anonymous mngallery state structs used in this PR.
Search for commits using the MWCC reference suggested by ribbanya.
Search for follow-up header cleanups around gm_801636D8, gm_80163838, gm_80163A3C, grCorneria_801DEC94, and lbAudioAx_80026F2C.
Search for later mngallery.c PRs that complete remaining .sdata2 or gallery data matches.
